files.patch:
@@ -135,10 +135,35 @@ jobs:
           NODE
           cat plan.json
 
+      - name: Publish planning status
+        if: ${{ github.event_name != 'workflow_dispatch' || github.event.inputs.apply_existing != 'true' }}
+        working-directory: clawsweeper
+        run: |
+          git config user.name "github-actions[bot]"
+          git config user.email "41898282+github-actions[bot]@users.noreply.github.com"
+          npm run dashboard
+          npm run status -- \
+            --state "Review in progress" \
+            --detail "Planned ${{ steps.select.outputs.planned_count }} items across ${{ steps.select.outputs.planned_shards }} shards. Capacity is ${{ steps.select.outputs.planned_capacity }} items. Review shards are starting; publish will merge artifacts when they finish." \
+            --run-url "https://github.com/${{ github.repository }}/actions/runs/${{ github.run_id }}"
+          git add README.md
+          if git diff --cached --quiet; then
+            echo "No status changes"
+          else
+            git commit -m "chore: mark sweep review in progress"
+            for attempt in 1 2 3; do
+              if git push; then
+                exit 0
+              fi
+              git pull --rebase --autostash
+            done
+            git push
+          fi
+
   review:
     needs: plan
     runs-on: ubuntu-latest
-    timeout-minutes: 360
+    timeout-minutes: 75
     strategy:
       fail-fast: false
       matrix:
@@ -257,7 +282,12 @@ jobs:
       - name: Apply review artifacts
         env:
           GH_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.OPENCLAW_GH_TOKEN || github.token }}
-        run: npm run apply-artifacts -- --artifact-dir artifacts
+        run: |
+          npm run apply-artifacts -- --artifact-dir artifacts
+          npm run status -- \
+            --state "Review publish complete" \
+            --detail "Merged review artifacts for run ${{ github.run_id }}. Dashboard reflects the latest files and proposed closes from completed shards." \
+            --run-url "https://github.com/${{ github.repository }}/actions/runs/${{ github.run_id }}"
 
       - name: Commit dashboard and review files
         run: |
@@ -326,6 +356,28 @@ jobs:
           git config user.name "github-actions[bot]"
           git config user.email "41898282+github-actions[bot]@users.noreply.github.com"
           mkdir -p .artifacts/apply-reports
+          publish_changes() {
+            message="$1"
+            git add README.md items closed apply-report.json
+            if git diff --cached --quiet; then
+              echo "No changes for: $message"
+              return 0
+            fi
+            git commit -m "$message"
+            for attempt in 1 2 3; do
+              if git push; then
+                return 0
+              fi
+              git pull --rebase --autostash
+            done
+            git push
+          }
+          npm run dashboard
+          npm run status -- \
+            --state "Apply in progress" \
+            --detail "Starting apply run for up to $limit fresh $apply_kind closes. Checkpoints commit every $checkpoint_size fresh closes; close delay is ${close_delay_ms}ms." \
+            --run-url "https://github.com/${{ github.repository }}/actions/runs/${{ github.run_id }}"
+          publish_changes "chore: mark sweep apply in progress"
           closed_total=0
           checkpoint=0
           while [ "$closed_total" -lt "$limit" ]; do
@@ -359,19 +411,11 @@ jobs:
             result_count="$(node -e 'const fs=require("node:fs"); const r=JSON.parse(fs.readFileSync(".artifacts/apply-reports/apply-report-" + process.argv[1] + ".json", "utf8")); console.log(r.length)' "$checkpoint")"
             closed_total=$((closed_total + closed_in_chunk))
             echo "Checkpoint $checkpoint result_count=$result_count closed_in_chunk=$closed_in_chunk closed_total=$closed_total/$limit"
-            git add README.md items closed apply-report.json
-            if git diff --cached --quiet; then
-              echo "No apply changes in checkpoint $checkpoint"
-            else
-              git commit -m "chore: apply sweep decisions checkpoint $checkpoint"
-              for attempt in 1 2 3; do
-                if git push; then
-                  break
-                fi
-                git pull --rebase --autostash
-              done
-              git push
-            fi
+            npm run status -- \
+              --state "Apply in progress" \
+              --detail "Checkpoint $checkpoint finished. Fresh closes in checkpoint: $closed_in_chunk. Total fresh closes in this run: $closed_total/$limit. Result records in checkpoint: $result_count." \
+              --run-url "https://github.com/${{ github.repository }}/actions/runs/${{ github.run_id }}"
+            publish_changes "chore: apply sweep decisions checkpoint $checkpoint"
             echo "::endgroup::"
             if [ "$result_count" -eq 0 ]; then
               echo "No more applicable proposed closes found."
@@ -382,6 +426,12 @@ jobs:
               break
             fi
           done
+          npm run dashboard
+          npm run status -- \
+            --state "Apply finished" \
+            --detail "Apply run finished with $closed_total fresh closes out of requested limit $limit. See apply-report.json for per-item results." \
+            --run-url "https://github.com/${{ github.repository }}/actions/runs/${{ github.run_id }}"
+          publish_changes "chore: mark sweep apply finished"
 
       - name: Commit apply results
         run: |

@@ -73,9 +73,9 @@ Each review job:
 
 Codex runs without GitHub write tokens. The runner checks the OpenClaw checkout before every review, makes the checkout read-only in CI, checks it again after review, and fails the item if Codex leaves any tracked or untracked change behind.
 
-Parallel workflow shards only receive planned item numbers. The final job merges artifacts and updates this README so the dashboard reflects progress. If the planner filled the current worker capacity, the publish job dispatches the next proposal-only sweep automatically.
+Parallel workflow shards only receive planned item numbers. The planner posts a best-effort workflow status note to this README before shards start, each shard emits `[review]` progress lines, and the final job merges artifacts and updates the dashboard. Review jobs time out after 75 minutes so one stuck shard cannot hold the review concurrency group indefinitely. If the planner filled the current worker capacity, the publish job dispatches the next proposal-only sweep automatically.
 
-To close later without rerunning Codex, dispatch the workflow with `apply_existing=true`. That mode reads existing `items/*.md`, refetches the issue/PR context, recomputes the snapshot hash, and only comments/closes if nothing changed since the proposal was written. Successfully closed or already-closed items move to `closed/<number>.md`; `items/` stays focused on open items that still need tracking. Apply runs update the dashboard when each checkpoint commits, cap total processed items separately from fresh closes, leave enough scan room to skip changed or already-closed records while still reaching fresh closures, and emit `[apply]` progress lines during long close batches. Apply mode defaults to `apply_min_age_days=0`, `apply_kind=issue`, a 15-second close delay, 50 fresh closes per checkpoint commit, and long retry backoff for GitHub secondary write throttling, so issue cleanup can apply high-confidence closes regardless of age while PRs remain excluded.
+To close later without rerunning Codex, dispatch the workflow with `apply_existing=true`. That mode reads existing `items/*.md`, refetches the issue/PR context, recomputes the snapshot hash, and only comments/closes if nothing changed since the proposal was written. Successfully closed or already-closed items move to `closed/<number>.md`; `items/` stays focused on open items that still need tracking. Apply runs update the dashboard when each checkpoint commits, cap total processed items separately from fresh closes, leave enough scan room to skip changed or already-closed records while still reaching fresh closures, and emit `[apply]` progress lines during long close batches. Apply mode also posts best-effort start/checkpoint/finish notes to the dashboard. Apply mode defaults to `apply_min_age_days=0`, `apply_kind=issue`, a 15-second close delay, 50 fresh closes per checkpoint commit, and long retry backoff for GitHub secondary write throttling, so issue cleanup can apply high-confidence closes regardless of age while PRs remain excluded.
 
 Maintainer-authored items are never auto-closed. Candidate planning and apply mode both read GitHub's `author_association` field and exclude `OWNER`, `MEMBER`, and `COLLABORATOR` items from automated close actions.

@@ -10,6 +10,7 @@
     "apply-artifacts": "node dist/clawsweeper.js apply-artifacts",
     "apply-decisions": "node dist/clawsweeper.js apply-decisions",
     "dashboard": "node dist/clawsweeper.js dashboard",
+    "status": "node dist/clawsweeper.js status",
     "lint": "oxlint src --tsconfig tsconfig.json",
     "format": "oxfmt --write src package.json tsconfig.json .oxfmtrc.json schema .github/workflows",
     "format:check": "oxfmt --check src package.json tsconfig.json .oxfmtrc.json schema .github/workflows",
